framework.name: NCSC Supply Chain Security Guidance
framework.scope: 12 principles to help organisations establish effective control over their supply chain, covering people, process, and technology aspects of supply chain security
framework.structure_description: 12 principles grouped into 4 stages: understand the risks, establish control, check your arrangements, continuous improvement

controls.title: Understand what needs to be protected and why
controls.title_nl: Understand what needs to be protected and why
controls.description: Before engaging with suppliers, understand what information or services you need to protect and why. Identify the assets most at risk from supply chain compromise and the potential business impact of their loss or compromise.
controls.description_nl: Before engaging with suppliers, understand what information or services you need to protect and why. Identify the assets most at risk from supply chain compromise and the potential business impact of their loss or compromise.
controls.implementation_guidance: Catalogue assets that suppliers can access or affect. Assess the business impact of compromise for each asset. Document the supply chain attack surface.
controls.verification_guidance: Review asset catalogue for completeness. Verify business impact assessments are current.

controls.title: Know who your suppliers are and build an understanding of what their security looks like
controls.title_nl: Know who your suppliers are and build an understanding of what their security looks like
controls.description: Map your supply chain to understand the full chain of suppliers, including sub-contractors and fourth parties. Assess the security posture of your suppliers and understand how they protect your information.
controls.description_nl: Map your supply chain to understand the full chain of suppliers, including sub-contractors and fourth parties. Assess the security posture of your suppliers and understand how they protect your information.
controls.implementation_guidance: Maintain a register of all suppliers with access to your systems or data. Include sub-contractors. Assess and record the security maturity of each supplier.
controls.verification_guidance: Review supplier register for completeness. Verify security assessments exist for critical suppliers.

controls.title: Understand the security risk posed by your supply chain
controls.title_nl: Understand the security risk posed by your supply chain
controls.description: Assess the specific security risks that your suppliers introduce, considering the type of access they have, the sensitivity of the information involved, and the potential impact of a supplier being compromised.
controls.description_nl: Assess the specific security risks that your suppliers introduce, considering the type of access they have, the sensitivity of the information involved, and the potential impact of a supplier being compromised.
controls.implementation_guidance: Conduct risk assessments for each supplier relationship. Consider the type and level of access, data sensitivity, and business dependency. Include supply chain risks in the enterprise risk register.
controls.verification_guidance: Review supply chain risk assessments. Verify risks are recorded in the enterprise risk register.

controls.title: Communicate your security needs to your suppliers
controls.title_nl: Communicate your security needs to your suppliers
controls.description: Clearly define and communicate your security requirements to suppliers. These should be proportionate to the risk and embedded in contracts and service level agreements.
controls.description_nl: Clearly define and communicate your security requirements to suppliers. These should be proportionate to the risk and embedded in contracts and service level agreements.
controls.implementation_guidance: Define minimum security standards for suppliers. Include security requirements in procurement documents, contracts, and SLAs. Communicate expectations clearly during onboarding.
controls.verification_guidance: Review contracts and SLAs for security requirements. Verify supplier onboarding includes security briefing.

controls.title: Set and communicate minimum security requirements for your suppliers
controls.title_nl: Set and communicate minimum security requirements for your suppliers
controls.description: Define minimum security requirements proportionate to the risk of each supplier relationship. Consider requiring Cyber Essentials certification or equivalent for suppliers handling sensitive information.
controls.description_nl: Define minimum security requirements proportionate to the risk of each supplier relationship. Consider requiring Cyber Essentials certification or equivalent for suppliers handling sensitive information.
controls.implementation_guidance: Define tiered security requirements based on supplier risk classification. Require Cyber Essentials at minimum for IT suppliers. Consider Cyber Essentials Plus or ISO 27001 for high-risk suppliers.
controls.verification_guidance: Review supplier security requirements by tier. Verify certification evidence is collected and current.

controls.title: Build security considerations into your contracting processes
controls.title_nl: Build security considerations into your contracting processes
controls.description: Include specific, measurable security requirements in contracts. Cover incident notification, data handling, access controls, audit rights, and sub-contractor management. Define consequences for non-compliance.
controls.description_nl: Include specific, measurable security requirements in contracts. Cover incident notification, data handling, access controls, audit rights, and sub-contractor management. Define consequences for non-compliance.
controls.implementation_guidance: Include security schedules in all supplier contracts. Specify incident notification timeframes, data handling requirements, audit rights, and sub-contractor approval processes.
controls.verification_guidance: Review sample contracts for security clauses. Verify all key security requirements are addressed.

controls.title: Meet your own security responsibilities as a supplier and consumer
controls.title_nl: Meet your own security responsibilities as a supplier and consumer
controls.description: Recognise that you are also a supplier in other organisations supply chains. Apply the same security standards you expect from your suppliers to your own operations. Security is a two-way responsibility.
controls.description_nl: Recognise that you are also a supplier in other organisations supply chains. Apply the same security standards you expect from your suppliers to your own operations. Security is a two-way responsibility.
controls.implementation_guidance: Assess your own security posture against the standards you require of suppliers. Address any gaps. Maintain certifications requested by your customers.
controls.verification_guidance: Compare internal security posture against supplier requirements. Verify no hypocrisy gaps.

controls.title: Raise awareness of security within your supply chain
controls.title_nl: Raise awareness of security within your supply chain
controls.description: Promote security awareness across the supply chain through training, guidance, and sharing of threat intelligence. Support suppliers in improving their security posture where appropriate.
controls.description_nl: Promote security awareness across the supply chain through training, guidance, and sharing of threat intelligence. Support suppliers in improving their security posture where appropriate.
controls.implementation_guidance: Share relevant threat intelligence with key suppliers. Provide security guidance and training materials. Consider running joint security exercises.
controls.verification_guidance: Review supplier communication logs. Verify threat intelligence sharing arrangements.

controls.title: Build assurance activities into your approach
controls.title_nl: Build assurance activities into your approach
controls.description: Verify that your suppliers are meeting their security obligations through audit, assessment, testing, or certification review. Assurance activities should be proportionate to the risk of the supplier relationship.
controls.description_nl: Verify that your suppliers are meeting their security obligations through audit, assessment, testing, or certification review. Assurance activities should be proportionate to the risk of the supplier relationship.
controls.implementation_guidance: Define an assurance programme for suppliers based on risk tier. Include questionnaires, on-site audits, certification review, and technical testing as appropriate.
controls.verification_guidance: Review supplier assurance programme. Verify assurance activities are conducted per schedule.

controls.title: Encourage the continuous improvement of security within your supply chain
controls.title_nl: Encourage the continuous improvement of security within your supply chain
controls.description: Security threats evolve constantly. Encourage and support suppliers in continuously improving their security posture. Use regular reviews and feedback to drive improvement.
controls.description_nl: Security threats evolve constantly. Encourage and support suppliers in continuously improving their security posture. Use regular reviews and feedback to drive improvement.
controls.implementation_guidance: Include security improvement objectives in supplier reviews. Share best practice guidance. Recognise suppliers who demonstrate security improvements.
controls.verification_guidance: Review supplier improvement plans. Verify progress against improvement objectives.

controls.title: Build trust with your suppliers
controls.description: Effective supply chain security requires trust between organisations. Build collaborative relationships where security information can be shared openly and issues can be raised without penalty.
controls.description_nl: Effective supply chain security requires trust between organisations. Build collaborative relationships where security information can be shared openly and issues can be raised without penalty.
controls.implementation_guidance: Establish regular security forums with key suppliers. Create safe channels for reporting security issues. Build security improvement into the relationship rather than using it only for enforcement.
controls.verification_guidance: Review supplier engagement activities. Verify trust-building mechanisms are in place.

controls.title: Plan for the unexpected
controls.description: Despite best efforts, supply chain security incidents will occur. Plan for supplier compromise, insolvency, or withdrawal of service. Maintain incident response plans that address supply chain scenarios.
controls.description_nl: Despite best efforts, supply chain security incidents will occur. Plan for supplier compromise, insolvency, or withdrawal of service. Maintain incident response plans that address supply chain scenarios.
controls.implementation_guidance: Develop contingency plans for critical supplier failure. Include supply chain breach scenarios in incident response plans. Test plans through exercises.
controls.verification_guidance: Review supply chain contingency plans. Verify exercise inclusion of supply chain scenarios.
